title: NASA's historic Artemis II lunar mission blasts off
subhed: NASA's team, which successfully entered Earth's orbit on Wednesday, includes Americans Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover and Christina Koch along with Canadian Jeremy Hansen. The astronauts' approximate 10-day mission will see them hurtle around Earth's natural satellite without landing, much like Apollo 8 did in 1968.

subhed: The global economic destabilization triggered by the war in the Middle East is now affecting the world's factory, which is struggling to offload its overproduction, writes columnist Harold Thibault.

title: Pesticides and cancer: For the first time, researchers find a connection at the national level
subhed: French researchers have developed a model to map pesticide exposure across Peru to reported cancer clusters. They demonstrate a 'robust spatial association' between these chemicals and increased cancer incidence rates in over 400 areas.

title: 'The Drama': A marriage tested by unsettling truths
subhed: Zendaya, radiating vulnerability, and Robert Pattinson, perpetually awkward, deliver an endearing portrayal of a couple whose relationship is about to be shattered by unexpected revelations.

title: Mystery among the gorillas: Who killed Dian Fossey?
subhed: More than 40 years after the murder of the primatologist at her camp in Rwanda, the existence of a piece of evidence, possibly kept in a laboratory of the French police, could revive the investigation.

Joël Soudron, one of France's most notorious drug traffickers, is arrested in Panama
subhed: Soudron was the subject of an Interpol Red Notice and two arrest warrants. He had been sought by the French National Fugitive Search Brigade since his escape in 2018.

title: War is transforming Ukraine's dogs who live on the front line
subhed: Canines found near combat zones are small, light and dark-colored, according to a study. These characteristics have helped them survive in a hostile environment.

title: Ukraine strikes Russia's lifeblood by targeting oil ports
subhed: Long-range Ukrainian drones hit three key infrastructures for Russian crude oil exports, as Kyiv aims to limit Moscow's windfall from surging oil prices.
